description: A moody, dark-toned photography blog with bold visuals

hero.heading: Light tells the story. I just press the shutter.
hero.subheading: A photography journal exploring shadows, contrast, and the beauty found in low light. New work published weekly.

items.title: After dark
items.description: Candid moments captured in urban landscapes when the sun goes down. The city reveals its second face.

items.title: Slow light
items.description: Light trails, star tracks, and the passage of time made visible through long exposure techniques.

items.title: Faces in shadow
items.description: Dramatic portraiture using natural and artificial light sources. Finding character in contrast.

cta.heading: See the world differently
cta.subheading: New prints and stories published weekly. Follow the journey.

pull_quote.quote: Photography is the story I fail to put into words.

testimonial.quote: The prints arrived beautifully packaged. The long exposure of the Tokyo crossing is even more stunning in person.
testimonial.author: Sarah M.

bio.name: James Chen
bio.statement: I photograph the moments between moments — the light that disappears in a breath, the expression that lasts half a second. My camera is how I pay attention.

about_page.heading: Behind the lens
paragraphs: I'm a photographer based in Portland, drawn to the moments most people walk past — the way light falls across a wet street, the shadows in a crowded market, the stillness of an empty room at dusk.
My work spans street photography, long exposure, and portraiture. I shoot primarily on location, working with available light whenever possible.
If you're interested in prints, editorial assignments, or just want to say hello — I'd love to hear from you.

sample_post.title: Thirty seconds on a rooftop in Tokyo
sample_post.excerpt: A long exposure experiment above the Shibuya crossing, where patience meets chaos.
paragraphs: I set up the tripod at 11pm, when the foot traffic below was at its densest. The goal was simple: capture the tension between the stillness of the buildings and the blur of human movement.
What I didn't expect was the way the neon reflections would streak across the frame, creating ribbons of color that felt almost painted.
Sometimes the best shots come from the moments you didn't plan for.

four_oh_four.heading: Nothing to see here — just darkness
four_oh_four.message: The page you're looking for has vanished into the shadows. Head back to the gallery and find something worth looking at.

footer.tagline: Documenting the world through a dark lens since 2019.
